rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Permutation test for trait-specific module recurrence

Source: R/tag_permutation.R
tag_permutation.Rd

Tests whether HOGs recur in trait-specific modules across independent species pairs more than expected by chance. The null shuffles trait labels across species (preserving marginal frequencies), re-tags species-specific modules, and counts HOG recurrence under the permuted labelling.

tag_permutation(
  classification,
  modules,
  orthologs,
  pairs,
  group,
  target_group,
  n_perm = 1000L,
  min_recurrence = 2L
)

Arguments

classification

Data frame from compare_modules_paired()$classification. Must contain columns pair_name, module, species ("sp1" or "sp2"), and classification.

modules

Named list of detect_modules outputs. Names must include all species referenced by pairs.

orthologs

Data frame with columns Species1, Species2, hog.

pairs

Data frame with columns sp1, sp2, and pair_name, matching the contrasts in classification.

group

Named character vector mapping species identifiers to trait values (e.g., c(BDIS = "annual", BSYL = "perennial")). All species in pairs must have entries.

target_group

Character string: the trait value to test recurrence for (e.g., "annual").

n_perm

Number of permutations (default 1000).

min_recurrence

Minimum number of pairs in which a HOG must appear to be counted as recurring (default 2).

Value

A list with components:

observed

Integer: number of HOGs recurring in >= min_recurrence pairs for target_group.

null_distribution

Integer vector of length n_perm: recurrence counts under the null.

p_value

One-sided p-value (conservative): (sum(null >= observed) + 1) / (n_perm + 1).

recurrence_table

Data frame with columns hog and n_pairs: observed per-HOG recurrence counts (only HOGs appearing in at least 1 pair).

target_group

Echo of the input.

min_recurrence

Echo of the input.

n_perm

Echo of the input.

Null model

Each permutation randomly reassigns trait labels to species, preserving the number of species per trait value. Module structure and gene content are held fixed — only the trait-to-species mapping changes. A pair contributes HOGs for target_group only when exactly one of its two species carries that trait under the (permuted) labelling. If both or neither species carry the target trait, the pair contributes nothing. This ensures the test is sensitive to the trait-species association, not to module size or gene overlap alone.

The test generalises to any number of trait values with arbitrary frequencies.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
mod_results <- compare_modules_paired(modules, orthologs,
  pairs = data.frame(sp1 = c("BDIS", "HVUL"),
                     sp2 = c("BSYL", "HJUB"),
                     pair_name = c("Brachypodium", "Hordeum")),
  group = c(BDIS = "annual", BSYL = "perennial",
            HVUL = "annual", HJUB = "perennial"))

result <- tag_permutation(
  mod_results$classification, modules, orthologs,
  pairs = data.frame(sp1 = c("BDIS", "HVUL"),
                     sp2 = c("BSYL", "HJUB"),
                     pair_name = c("Brachypodium", "Hordeum")),
  group = c(BDIS = "annual", BSYL = "perennial",
            HVUL = "annual", HJUB = "perennial"),
  target_group = "annual"
)
result$p_value
} # }
